meta.last_updated: 2026-05-21 (PRD-089 / EXEC-089 Phase 2.5 in progress — WS1_LOG + WS2_RETENTION implementation complete; WS3_SIGNOFF authored WAVE-2-SIGN-OFF.md; WS4_GOVERNANCE tracker reconciliation applied (this update); PROD-ANCHOR-STD-001 ratified)
meta.update_rule: Update both this file and WAVE-2-PROGRESS-TRACKER.md together when phase state changes. This file is the machine-readable projection of the .md; it does not own truth. Update parent ROLLOUT-TRACKER.json cursor when Wave 2 active_phase changes.
meta.reading_order: cursor → direction → phases → deferred_register → validation_matrix → open_decisions. Read roadmap_ref for phase deliverables and exit gates. Read parent_tracker_ref for Wave 1 history and Wave 2 entry criteria.

principles: Transport before consumers — no projection consumer PRD may be initiated until its required producer path exists and I1–I4 are verified. Phase 2.3 does not start before Phase 2.1 gates.

Producers expand one category at a time — Class A adjustment ships separately from Dependency Events. Within a category, intra-category parity applies: fills and credits are a symmetric Dependency Event rollout pair (ADR-055 / RULE-6 / FIB-H §G) and must land in the same slice.
ADR-055 parity is not relaxed — the exemplar proved Class A and Class B simultaneously. Subsequent expansion slices must still satisfy parity within each slice boundary.

Consumers are projection-only — no consumer slice may write to PFT, the grind authoring store, or any other authoring table. Consumers write only to projection stores and processed_messages. origin_label travels from authoring boundary to consumer unchanged.
Gates are pass/fail — each phase has a hard exit condition. No partial advancement. Phase N+1 PRD must land before Phase N+1 code begins.

No scope expansion without FIB amendment — FIB-S downstream_expansion_allowed_without_amendment: false applies to every phase. Any new operator-visible outcome, external consumer contract, or new surface not in the containment loop requires a FIB amendment.
Freeze discipline holds — ADR-052 through ADR-056 are frozen. If a phase surfaces a real conflict, write a superseding ADR. Do not patch frozen docs silently.

Parallelization rule (effective 2026-05-19, post Phase 2.2) — Phase 2.3a (Operational Outbox Observability) and Phase 2.3 (Lifecycle-Aware Completeness Projection) may proceed in parallel. They operate on different architectural layers: 2.3a is a read-only runtime verification surface; 2.3 is the first projection consumer with derived completeness state. Parallelization is permitted while: (1) relay topology is frozen, (2) replay ordering semantics are unchanged, (3) no projection logic is introduced into the observability surface, (4) no write/replay/repair actions are added to the admin observability boundary. If any condition breaks, parallelization reverts to sequential. This marks the transition from transport stabilization to bounded operationalization of a stabilized propagation substrate.
direction.execution_protocol: Every phase runs: /prd-writer → /lead-architect (EXEC-SPEC scaffold) → /build PRD-###. /build-pipeline dispatches domain-expert skills per workstream. No skill is invoked directly outside the build-pipeline chain. See WAVE-2-ROLLOUT-MAP.md §3.
direction.phase_transition_rule: Exiting Phase N → Opening Phase N+1 requires two documents: (1) Phase N exit gate met, (2) Phase N+1 PRD drafted and approved. No code written for Phase N+1 before its PRD lands.
non_goals: No CDC / WAL relay (Debezium, pg_logical, WAL streaming) — post-pilot scale upgrade
No external consumer contract — no public event bus, no third-party event semantics, no reconciliation interface
No event sourcing — outbox does not reconstruct authoritative state
No authoritative totals — no Total Drop, shift-end settlement, or final money position
No multi-consumer fan-out registry — Wave 2 assumes a single internal consumer path
No UI-driven reconciliation — render completeness labels; never recompute financial state against authoring stores
No compliance domain scope — mtl_entry / MTLService remains parallel and isolated
No new PFT columns — player_financial_transaction is append-only
wave_2_entry_criteria: I1–I4 harness proven against real implementation — met 2026-05-11 (EXEC-081)

transport_bugs_fixed.root_cause: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — player_financial_transaction_no_updates (USING=false) rejects DO UPDATE on conflict-resolution scan path even without a real conflict (PostgreSQL 17 behavior with partial index targets)

transport_bugs_fixed.root_cause: Migration 20260511134257 added event_type NOT NULL to table_buyin_telemetry but did not update this AFTER INSERT trigger

transport_bugs_fixed.root_cause: Migration 20260511134129 assumed SECURITY DEFINER RPCs for all outbox writes, but rpc_create_financial_txn is SECURITY INVOKER (ADR-040); no INSERT policy = default deny for authenticated role

phases.label: Operational Outbox Observability

parallelization.rationale: Phase 2.3a is a read-only runtime verification surface for the existing transactional substrate. Phase 2.3 introduces the first projection consumer. They operate on different architectural layers and have no shared write surface. Phase 2.3a does not mutate producer semantics, relay behavior, replay ordering, processed_messages, or projection state.
parallelization_conditions: no projection logic introduced into the observability surface
no write/replay/repair actions added to the admin observability boundary
parallelization.reverts_to_sequential_if: any parallelization condition above is violated

phases.scope: Read-only internal admin surface at /admin/outbox-observability. Makes finance_outbox row state and relay delivery status inspectable without SQL. Relay health summary, event queue with full semantic envelope, poison candidate labeling (delivery_attempts >= 3, non-authoritative pilot heuristic), and search/filter. Does not build projection consumers, write to any authoring store, or process events.

key_decisions: DEC-EXEC-1: rpc_process_operational_projection stamps processed_at atomically — no separate rpc_acknowledge_outbox_delivery call for operational branch

DEC-EXEC-5: completeness uses 5-step logic: unknown / complete-zero / partial-no-lifecycle / partial-with-backlog / complete
DEC-EXEC-6: operationalBacklog split into claimable (delivery_attempts < 5) and deadLetter (>= 5) in observability route
DEC-EXEC-7: uuidFormat (permissive regex) used in operationalProjectionQuerySchema, not z.string().uuid() (RFC 4122 enforcement rejected per project convention)
DEC-EXEC-8: completeness backlog check is TABLE-SCOPED (casino_id + gaming_day + table_id), not casino-wide

phases.scope: Closing slice of Wave 2: (1) structured outbox_relay_cycle log line per relay invocation (FR-1 authenticated + FR-2 unauthenticated variants) with backlog split by fact_class and claimability plus per-cycle lag aggregates; (2) cron-driven 7-day retention path (rpc_cleanup_outbox_processed + supporting partial index + GET cleanup route + daily Vercel cron) for processed finance_outbox rows; (3) WAVE-2-SIGN-OFF.md ratifying PROD-ANCHOR-STD-001 and reconciling post-Wave-2 backlog against PRD-088 closure (PWB-003 CLOSED; PWB-001 / PWB-002 / W2-OBS-CASHOUT-PRODUCER-001 carried forward). No new producers, consumers, or UI surfaces.
audit_patches_applied: P0-PHASE-24-EVIDENCE-GATE — Phase 2.4 closure evidence verified (PRD-088 / EXEC-088 files present, checkpoint complete, commit 931f5ed9, residual gap classification): all 5 items PASS before WS3 / WS4 dispatch
P1-TEST-GATE-ORDERING — test-pass moved into Phase 1 gates; WS3 / WS4 documentation workstreams require WS1_LOG + WS2_RETENTION implementation gates to pass first

P1-LAG-SAMPLE-CLOCK-CONTRACT — lag samples DB-clock derived from (processed_at - created_at) timestamps, not app-server Date.now(); duplicate/skipped/failed/claim-error/auth-fail outcomes excluded by construction; orchestrator-accepted TS-subtraction deviation preserves the DB-clock invariant (no app-server clock participates)

P1-BACKLOG-CLAIMABILITY-DEFINITION — backlog count predicates follow rpc_claim_class_a_outbox_batch and rpc_claim_operational_outbox_batch claim predicates verbatim including the operational event_type whitelist drift detected during implementation

P1-RETENTION-REPLAY-BOUNDARY-GATE — WAVE-2-SIGN-OFF.md §8.2 contains the three required assertions (boundary stated verbatim; authoring-store reseed escape hatch naming player_financial_transaction / pit_cash_observation / table_buyin_telemetry / table_fills / table_credits; FIB/ADR amendment requirement for unbounded replay)

P2-EXPLAIN-INDEX-BRITTLENESS — EXPLAIN-index test split into deterministic index-exists + predicate-alignment assertions plus advisory planner-evidence test behind RUN_PLANNER_EVIDENCE_TESTS env gate (10k+1k row seed not run on default CI)
deliverables: outbox_relay_cycle structured log line — FR-1 authenticated variant with backlog split by fact_class and claimability + per-cycle processing-lag aggregates min/p50/p95/max; FR-2 unauthenticated variant for auth_fail invocations with backlog null and lag nulls — app/api/internal/outbox-relay/route.ts (GET + POST share runRelayCycle)

Branch lag-sample contract: lagSamplesMs: number[] on Class A and operational consumer branch results; DB-clock derived (Date.parse(processed_at) - Date.parse(created_at)) for processedEventIds only; duplicate/skipped/failed/claim-error/auth-fail outcomes excluded by construction (P1-LAG-SAMPLE-CLOCK-CONTRACT honored; orchestrator-accepted TS-subtraction deviation, DB-clock invariant preserved)

dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.finding: .env (loaded by jest.setup.js with override:false) contains REMOTE Supabase URL/keys; .env.local contains LOCAL URL/keys. RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true npx jest hits remote instance which lacks unmerged migrations and fails finance_outbox-touching integration tests. To run against local DB, tests must be invoked with explicit local env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:54321 + LOCAL anon/service keys).
dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.recommended_remediation: Either (a) flip jest.setup.js to prefer .env.local over .env in test runs, (b) document explicit-override requirement in QA-006 / QA-001 integration test playbook, or (c) introduce .env.test holding LOCAL URL/keys with jest.setup.js loading it first.
dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.rationale: WS3 / WS4 are documentation workstreams that do not run integration tests, so dispatch is not blocked. Pre-merge DoD (PRD-089 §8) must include either fix or explicit documentation.

governance_rules.phase_entry: Every phase requires PRD drafted & approved → EXEC-SPEC scaffolded → /build-pipeline executed to exit criteria. Tracker checkbox completion does NOT authorize starting the next phase.
governance_rules.skill_dispatch: Skills are dispatched by /build-pipeline, not invoked directly. See WAVE-2-ROLLOUT-MAP.md §3.
governance_rules.parity_rule: ADR-055 is not relaxed. No asymmetric rollout. Fills and credits are a symmetric Dependency Event rollout pair — intra-category parity; they must land in the same slice. Dependency Events must carry origin_label: estimated — not upgradeable.
governance_rules.frozen_docs: ADR-052–056 are frozen. Violations require a superseding ADR — not patches to frozen docs.
governance_rules.i1_scope: I1 atomicity is producer-scoped. Every new producer must ship with an explicit atomicity proof test. The exemplar proof does not certify future producers.
governance_rules.i2_i3_i4_scope: I2–I4 are transport-substrate invariants. Proven once at relay/consumer architecture level. Re-verify only if relay worker, ordering guarantee, or idempotent-consumer implementation changes materially.
governance_rules.teardown_rule: PRD-082 harness teardown migration must be authored and applied before any Phase 2.1 migration merges. See TEARDOWN-ARTIFACT-PRD-082.md.
